title: Article 370 Abrogation Held Constitutional by Supreme Court
summary: A 5-judge Constitution Bench unanimously upheld the August 2019 abrogation of Article 370. The Court held J&K does not retain internal sovereignty distinct from India and directed assembly elections by 30 September 2024.

title: Supreme Court: Marital Rape Exception Hearing Continues
summary: The Supreme Court continued hearing petitions challenging Exception 2 to Section 375 IPC (now Section 63 BNS), which exempts marital rape from prosecution. Government opposes criminalisation citing institution of marriage.

title: Supreme Court Mandates POSH Compliance for All Workplaces
summary: The Supreme Court directed all Central and State Government departments, statutory authorities, and PSUs to constitute Internal Complaints Committees under the POSH Act, 2013. Non-compliance to attract personal liability for officers.

title: Aligarh Muslim University Granted Minority Status
summary: A 7-judge Constitution Bench held by 4:3 that AMU can claim minority status under Article 30. The Court overruled Azeez Basha (1968) and laid down a new test based on who establishes and administers the institution.

title: Same-Sex Marriage: Constitution Bench Declines Recognition
summary: A 5-judge Constitution Bench by 3:2 declined to recognize same-sex marriage under the Special Marriage Act. While refusing to read down marriage laws, all five judges affirmed LGBTQIA+ couples' right to choose partners. Issue referred to Parliament.

title: POCSO: Possession of CSAM Made Strict Liability Offence
summary: The Supreme Court held that mere viewing, possession, or storage of child sexual abuse material is an offence under Section 15 POCSO, even without intent to distribute. The Court directed legislative replacement of 'child pornography' with 'CSEAM'.

title: Supreme Court Strikes Down PIB Fact-Check Unit
summary: The Bombay High Court struck down the 2023 amendment to the IT Rules empowering the PIB Fact Check Unit, holding it violates Article 19(1)(a). The Supreme Court declined to stay the judgment.

title: RBI Permits 24x7 Real-Time Cross-Border Payments
summary: The RBI announced the rollout of 24x7 real-time cross-border payment infrastructure leveraging UPI. Pilot with Singapore (PayNow) successful; expansion to UAE, Bhutan, and Mauritius approved.

title: Supreme Court: Bail is the Rule, Even in PMLA Cases
summary: The Supreme Court held that the twin conditions for bail under Section 45 PMLA cannot be applied mechanically. Constitutional courts must consider incarceration period and right to speedy trial under Article 21.

title: Insolvency Code Amendment Bill, 2024 — Real Estate Insolvency
summary: Parliament passed amendments to the IBC, introducing project-wise insolvency for real estate developers. Homebuyers can now initiate insolvency proceedings against specific projects rather than the entire entity.

title: Supreme Court Upholds Sub-Classification of SC/STs
summary: A 7-judge Constitution Bench held by 6:1 that States are constitutionally permitted to sub-classify Scheduled Castes and Tribes for reservations. The Court overruled E.V. Chinnaiah (2005) and held that the creamy layer principle should apply to SC/ST reservations.

title: GST: E-Invoicing Mandatory for Turnover Above ₹5 Crore
summary: Effective 1 August 2024, e-invoicing under GST became mandatory for all B2B transactions where the supplier's aggregate turnover exceeds ₹5 crore in any preceding financial year. Earlier threshold was ₹10 crore.

title: Supreme Court: Royalty Not Tax, States Can Levy on Minerals
summary: A 9-judge Constitution Bench by 8:1 held royalty under MMDR Act is not a tax. State legislatures retain power to tax mineral rights and lands containing minerals. Overrules India Cement (1989). Allows retrospective levy from 1 April 2005.

title: MCA Notifies Cost Audit Threshold Revision
summary: The MCA revised cost audit thresholds under the Companies Act, 2013. Companies in regulated sectors with turnover above ₹100 crore now require mandatory cost audit. Industry-wise applicability table updated.

title: Delhi High Court: Anti-Trust Probe into Big Tech Continues
summary: The Delhi High Court refused to interfere with CCI's anti-trust investigation into Google's app store policies and dominant position in the Indian app market. CCI proceedings to continue.

title: GST Council Recommends 18% Slab for Most Services
summary: The 53rd GST Council meeting recommended consolidating service tax rates with most services moving to the 18% slab. Special rates retained for essential services, healthcare, and education. Changes effective from FY 2026-27.

title: Bombay High Court: WhatsApp Group Admins Not Liable for Members' Posts
summary: The Bombay High Court reaffirmed that WhatsApp group admins cannot be held vicariously liable for offensive posts by group members, absent specific evidence of knowledge or instigation.

title: Supreme Court: NCDRC Cannot Award Damages Without Consumer Complaint
summary: The Supreme Court held that the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission cannot suo motu award damages or initiate proceedings under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 without a complaint from the consumer or appropriate authority.

title: RBI Issues Master Direction on Digital Lending
summary: The RBI issued the Master Direction on Digital Lending, 2025, mandating regulated entities to ensure transparent loan offerings, prohibit auto-debit without consent, and provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS) before disbursement.

title: BNSS: Trial Process Digitised — Electronic FIR and E-Trial
summary: The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 introduces electronic FIR registration (e-FIR), digital evidence, video conferencing for witnesses, and time-bound investigation. Forensic investigation mandatory for offences punishable with 7+ years.

title: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) Comes Into Force Replacing IPC
summary: The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 came into force on 1 July 2024, replacing the Indian Penal Code, 1860. Along with the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (replacing CrPC) and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (replacing Indian Evidence Act), this represents the largest overhaul of Indian criminal law in over a century.

title: MCA Notifies Companies (Significant Beneficial Owner) Amendment Rules
summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs notified amendments to the SBO Rules, tightening disclosure of significant beneficial owners. Companies must now identify SBOs holding 10% or more (lowered from 25% in some cases) and file BEN-2 returns within 30 days.

title: ESG Disclosure Mandatory for Top 1000 Listed Companies
summary: SEBI made the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) mandatory for the top 1000 listed companies by market cap. Core BRSR reasonable assurance also mandated for top 150.

title: MSME Act Amendment: 45-Day Payment Strictly Enforced
summary: Amendments to Section 43B of the Income Tax Act require buyers from registered MSMEs to actually pay within 45 days; otherwise the deduction is disallowed in the year of accrual. Enforcement strict from FY 2024-25 onwards.

title: SEBI Tightens IPO Disclosure Norms for Pre-IPO Investors
summary: SEBI amended the ICDR Regulations to require enhanced disclosure of pre-IPO transactions, related party deals, and promoter lock-in periods. Issuers must disclose material developments in the 6 months preceding the IPO filing.

title: Four Labour Codes Notified — Operational from 1 April 2026
summary: The Ministry of Labour & Employment notified that all four Labour Codes — Code on Wages 2019, Industrial Relations Code 2020, Code on Social Security 2020, and OSH Code 2020 — will be fully operational from 1 April 2026. Key changes include the 50% wage rule for PF/gratuity calculation and gratuity eligibility for fixed-term employees from 1 year.

title: MeitY Issues Advisory on AI-Generated Content Labeling
summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT issued an advisory requiring intermediaries to label AI-generated content (deepfakes, synthetic media) clearly. Significant Social Media Intermediaries must implement detection mechanisms.

title: Electoral Bonds Scheme Struck Down as Unconstitutional
summary: The Supreme Court struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme, holding that anonymous corporate funding of political parties violates voters' right to information under Article 19(1)(a). SBI was directed to disclose all bond purchase and redemption details.

title: Income Tax Bill, 2025 Replaces Income Tax Act, 1961
summary: The Income Tax Bill, 2025, simplifying and consolidating six decades of amendments, was introduced. Key changes include simplified language, removal of redundant provisions, and modernised digital filing. Effective from AY 2026-27.

title: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Section 6A of the Citizenship Act
summary: A Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of Section 6A of the Citizenship Act, 1955, which grants citizenship to immigrants who entered Assam before March 25, 1971. The court ruled that the provision serves a humanitarian purpose and does not violate Article 14.

title: Delhi High Court Issues Guidelines on Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards
summary: The Delhi High Court issued comprehensive guidelines for enforcement of foreign arbitral awards under the New York Convention, streamlining the process and reducing delays. The court clarified the scope of public policy objections under Section 48 of the Arbitration Act.

title: SEBI Introduces New Framework for ESG Rating Providers
summary: SEBI has notified a comprehensive regulatory framework for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) rating providers. The framework mandates registration, transparency in methodology, management of conflicts of interest, and periodic disclosures by ESG rating agencies.

title: RBI Circular on Digital Lending: Key Compliance Requirements
summary: The Reserve Bank of India issued updated guidelines on digital lending, mandating that all loan disbursements and repayments occur directly between the borrower and the regulated entity's bank account. Third-party platforms are prohibited from handling loan funds.

title: Parliament Passes Digital Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Bill, 2026
summary: Parliament passed the Digital Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Bill, introducing stricter penalties for data breaches and expanding the Data Protection Board's powers. The amendment mandates that data fiduciaries appoint a Data Protection Officer within 90 days.

title: MCA Notifies Rules for Fast-Track Cross-Border Mergers
summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has notified new rules enabling fast-track cross-border mergers for companies with turnover below Rs 500 crore. The rules aim to simplify the process under the Companies Act, 2013 and reduce NCLT approval timelines.

title: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Arbitration Clause in Unstamped Agreements
summary: The Supreme Court in a landmark 7-judge bench decision clarified that an arbitration clause in an unstamped agreement is not void but unenforceable until proper stamp duty is paid. The court overruled the earlier N.N. Global decision.

title: CBDT Issues Clarification on Angel Tax Provisions for Startups
summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes issued a clarification stating that DPIIT-recognized startups with aggregate paid-up share capital not exceeding Rs 25 crore will be exempt from angel tax provisions under Section 56(2)(viib) of the Income Tax Act.

title: Bombay High Court Strikes Down Retrospective GST Notification
summary: The Bombay High Court struck down a retrospective GST notification that sought to levy tax on services provided by clubs and associations to their members. The court held that retrospective taxation violates Article 19(1)(g) of the Constitution.

title: SEBI Tightens Insider Trading Norms for Connected Persons
summary: SEBI has amended the Prohibition of Insider Trading Regulations to expand the definition of 'connected persons' and mandate structured digital databases for tracking access to unpublished price-sensitive information (UPSI).

title: New Labour Codes: Government Sets April 2026 Implementation Date
summary: The Ministry of Labour confirmed that the four new Labour Codes on Wages, Industrial Relations, Social Security, and Occupational Safety will be implemented from April 1, 2026, requiring all establishments to update employment contracts and HR policies.

title: RBI Increases UPI Transaction Limit for Tax Payments to Rs 5 Lakh
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has enhanced the per-transaction limit for UPI payments towards tax payments from Rs 1 lakh to Rs 5 lakh. The move aims to facilitate easier digital tax compliance for individuals and businesses.

title: Supreme Court Orders Mandatory Mediation Before Filing of Commercial Disputes
summary: In a significant ruling, the Supreme Court directed that all commercial disputes above Rs 1 crore must undergo mandatory pre-litigation mediation under the Commercial Courts Act before being filed in court, to reduce pendency.

title: MCA Simplifies LLP Annual Filing Process with New Form 11A
summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has introduced a simplified annual filing form (Form 11A) for LLPs with turnover below Rs 5 crore. The new form consolidates multiple compliance requirements into a single filing.

title: GST Council Recommends 18% Rate on Online Gaming; 28% on Casinos
summary: The 56th GST Council meeting recommended maintaining 18% GST on online skill-based gaming and 28% on casinos and betting. The Council also approved the inclusion of Extra Neutral Alcohol under GST with a 5% rate.

title: Karnataka High Court Declares IT Sector Layoffs Without Notice as Unfair Labour Practice
summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that mass layoffs in the IT sector without adequate notice or compensation constitute unfair labour practice under the Industrial Disputes Act. The ruling impacts several large IT companies in Bengaluru.

title: DPDPA Rules Notified: Data Localization Requirements for Critical Personal Data
summary: The government has notified rules under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act requiring that critical personal data (financial, health, biometric) be stored and processed within India. Companies have 180 days to comply.

title: SEBI Mandates T+0 Settlement for Top 500 Stocks from March 2026
summary: SEBI has issued a circular mandating same-day (T+0) settlement for the top 500 stocks by market capitalization from March 31, 2026. The move follows the successful implementation of T+1 settlement and aims to enhance market efficiency.

title: DPDP Rules, 2025 Notified — Implementation Begins
summary: The Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025 were notified, providing operational framework for the DPDP Act, 2023. Rules cover consent, breach notification within 72 hours, processing of children's data, and Data Fiduciary obligations. Phased implementation begins with significant data fiduciaries.

title: Government Introduces Bharatiya Sakshya (Amendment) Bill for Digital Evidence
summary: The government introduced the Bharatiya Sakshya (Amendment) Bill to strengthen provisions for admissibility of digital evidence, including blockchain records, IoT device data, and AI-generated documents in court proceedings.

title: Supreme Court Directs States to Establish Commercial Courts in Every District
summary: The Supreme Court directed all state governments to establish dedicated Commercial Courts in every district within 6 months, citing the need to expedite resolution of commercial disputes and improve India's ease of doing business ranking.

title: RBI Issues Master Direction on IT Governance for Regulated Entities
summary: The RBI issued comprehensive Master Directions on IT Governance and Cybersecurity for all regulated entities, mandating CISO appointment, annual IT audits, and incident reporting within 6 hours of a cyber incident.

title: Income Tax Department Launches New Faceless Assessment Portal 2.0
summary: The Income Tax Department launched the upgraded Faceless Assessment Portal 2.0 with AI-driven case selection, video conferencing for hearings, and real-time status tracking. The portal aims to reduce assessment completion time to 90 days.

title: Madras High Court Upholds Validity of Pre-Packaged Insolvency for MSMEs
summary: The Madras High Court upheld the constitutional validity of the pre-packaged insolvency resolution process for MSMEs under the IBC, rejecting challenges that it discriminated against operational creditors.

title: MCA Proposes Mandatory CSR Spending Audit for Companies Above Rs 100 Crore Turnover
summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has issued a draft notification proposing mandatory third-party audit of CSR spending for companies with turnover exceeding Rs 100 crore, aiming to ensure genuine compliance with Section 135 of the Companies Act.

title: Data Protection Board Issues First Penalty Under DPDPA for Healthcare Data Breach
summary: The Data Protection Board of India imposed its first penalty of Rs 50 crore on a leading healthcare platform for a data breach affecting 5 million users. The Board found violations of data minimization and security safeguard obligations.
